title: Phlix library query parameters
description: Schema describing the query parameters for the movie-list browse API endpoint (GET /api/v1/media). All parameters are optional — omitting a parameter means 'no filter on this dimension'. Multiple values in array parameters are OR-ed together (e.g. genres[]=Action&genres[]=Drama returns items that are Action OR Drama). Year ranges are inclusive. Sort defaults to name ASC when not specified.

search.description: Full-text or fuzzy search query applied to the media item name. Uses MySQL FULLTEXT with boolean fallback to LIKE %% matching.

genres.description: Filter to items that contain ANY of the listed genres (OR logic). Items with no genres set are excluded when this filter is applied.

yearFrom.description: Minimum release year (inclusive). Items with no year set are excluded when this filter is applied.

yearTo.description: Maximum release year (inclusive). Items with no year set are excluded when this filter is applied.

ratings.description: Filter to items with any of the listed content ratings (MPAA-style: G, PG, PG-13, R, NC-17, X, UNRATED). Items with no rating set are excluded when this filter is applied.

actors.description: Filter to items that feature ANY of the listed actors (partial name match on the actors array in metadata_json). Items with no actors set are excluded when this filter is applied.

sort.description: Sort field for the result set.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

offset.description: Number of items to skip for pagination (offset = page * limit).

libraryId.description: Scope the result (and its total) to a single library by id. Omitted/blank means an all-libraries query. Drives the per-library Browse rails and the dedicated library page.

parentId.description: Scope the result to the direct children of one media item by id — used to fetch the seasons/episodes of a series (or the episodes of a season) for the series detail page. Mutually exclusive with `topLevel`.

topLevel.description: When true, return only top-level items (those with no parent: movies and series), excluding seasons and episodes. Browse rails and library grids set this so a series library shows shows rather than a flat dump of every episode. Mutually exclusive with `parentId`; ignored when `search` is set so search still spans the whole library.
